invocation: Post-correctness-fixes validation (venue dead-join + hardcoded fielding alignment)

intervention.hypothesis: Two correctness bugs in the DML confounder matrix were silently degrading the de-confounding step: (1) `LEFT JOIN games g` joined against an empty dimension table, so `venue_code` was 0 for every PA (park effects feature dead), and (2) `if_shift` / `of_shift` were hardcoded to literal 0 instead of the real `pitches.if_/of_fielding_alignment` columns (97% fill, 5 distinct levels each). Researcher predicted ~+0.01 to +0.03 Spearman lift after fixing both.
fixes_applied: src/analytics/causal_war.py:_extract_pa_data -- dropped LEFT JOIN games g; pulls home_team / away_team / if_fielding_alignment / of_fielding_alignment directly from pitches via MAX() inside the PA-level GROUP BY (constant within a PA).

src/analytics/causal_war.py:_build_features -- venue_code now factor-encodes home_team (~30 distinct values, 100% fill) instead of the always-NULL games.venue; if_shift / of_shift now factor-encode the real fielding alignment categoricals.

gates.name: leakage_check
gates.measured: train 2015-2022 vs test 2023-2024 (disjoint by season range)

gates.name: spearman_rho_combined
gates.note: REGRESSION: combined Spearman fell below the 0.60 spec gate after correctness fixes.

gates.name: spearman_rho_batters
gates.note: Batter cohort Spearman dropped below 0.60 -- previously cleared at 0.6743.

gates.name: spearman_rho_pitchers
gates.note: Pitcher cohort essentially unchanged (-0.007); fixes are largely orthogonal to pitcher signal.

verdict: REGRESSION -- correctness fixes degraded test-set headline metrics
verdict_explanation: Both correctness bugs were real and the fixes are methodologically sound (live park-effects feature via pitches.home_team, real fielding-alignment categoricals from pitches.if_/of_fielding_alignment). However, the empirical effect on test-set headline metrics was the OPPOSITE of the researcher's +0.01 to +0.03 Spearman prediction: combined Spearman fell from 0.6314 to 0.4938 (-0.138), driven almost entirely by the batter cohort (-0.107). The pitcher cohort was effectively unchanged (-0.007). The DML test nuisance R^2 also flipped from +0.0007 to -0.0089, indicating the enriched confounder set generalises WORSE out-of-sample on batters. Likely root cause: factor-encoded home_team gives the train-set DML model 30 effective levels to memorise team-park-era confounding that does not transfer to the 2023-2024 holdout; combined with the new fielding-alignment factors, the residualisation is now over-correcting for batter context, deflating the per-player residual signal that drives downstream rank correlation with bWAR.
notes: Post-correctness-fixes run. Both bugs (dead games-table venue join at causal_war.py:803 and hardcoded if_shift/of_shift at causal_war.py:865-866) confirmed and patched in a single edit each. All 27 CausalWAR pytest cases pass. Re-trained on the canonical 2015-2022 window using HistGradientBoosting nuisance + 5-fold cross-fitting + 50 bootstraps; validated at pa_min=300 / ip_min=50 to match the 171420Z baseline. The methodological correctness improvements stand independent of the headline regression -- park effects and shifts are now measurable rather than silently dead -- but the prediction that fixing dead features would tighten downstream WAR-rank correlation did NOT hold. The headline regression should be flagged for follow-up investigation: candidates include (a) regularising the new categorical splits (target-encode home_team rather than label-encode to avoid 30-level over-fitting), (b) interacting fielding alignment with batted-ball context only, (c) re-evaluating whether richer confounders are the right Spearman lever vs the structural cross-position scale tension previously identified.
